title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, 22, a cancer survivor from Dedham, is running the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Taylor was diagnosed with an aggressive thyroid cancer at 19, but received pioneering treatment at Mass General.
Her marathon run honors her doctors, the AYA program, survivorship friends, and hospitalized patients feeling scared and alone.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 for Boston Medical Center's safety-net mission and social workers.
Boston Medical Center provides care regardless of patients' ability to pay, supported by social workers.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium, continuing their search for a first NWSL win.
Head coach Filipa Patão emphasized patience in attack, acknowledging her team's rushed play in the final third.
Amanda Gutierres, a key player, struggled with passing accuracy and has yet to score in four appearances for Legacy FC.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., a suspended Melrose attorney, was arraigned for embezzling client funds and practicing law while suspended.
Charges include embezzlement, larceny, and unauthorized law practice; Rappa allegedly misused $660,000 from a client's trust.
Rappa was ordered to surrender his passport; bail set at $2,500; advised to cease contact with witnesses.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued after a fiery crash at Bedford Toll Plaza, New Hampshire, by a state trooper and two security members.
The 51-year-old 'Bob's Burgers' actor was hospitalized with serious injuries but expressed gratitude towards rescuers and hospital staff.
The single-car crash is under investigation, and New Hampshire police are seeking information from the public.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb of Dover charged with assaulting a New Hampshire State Trooper in an unprovoked attack.
Incident occurred in a parking lot near Dover Point Road; Newcomb allegedly attempted to take the trooper's gun.
Newcomb faced misdemeanor and felony charges, including DWI and criminal threatening; both received minor injuries.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, is running the Boston Marathon to honor his second mother, Sharla Sieve, who died from cancer.
Mallett supports Mass General and a fundraiser raising over $123,000 to provide cancer treatment in Boston.
After barely completing last year's race due to injury, Mallett aims to finish the Boston Marathon again this year.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect Patriots to acquire Eagles WR A.J. Brown after June 1 due to salary cap considerations.
A.J. Brown's potential trade hinges on post-June 1 cap benefits, reducing Eagles' dead cap hit from $43M to $16.4M.
Patriots' WR roster remains a focus despite signing Romeo Doubs; acquiring Brown could enhance their offensive lineup.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Testament of Ann Lee' featuring Amanda Seyfried is streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' directed by Nia DaCosta is available on Netflix.
Ken Burns' documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' is streaming on PBS.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox won their home opener 5-2 against the San Diego Padres, breaking a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin ended an 0-for-19 slump with an RBI single, receiving boos and later cheers from Fenway Park fans.
Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing two runs with four hits, helping to secure the win for Red Sox at Fenway.
